# docs(agentos): MemoryCore.md to 9/10 — first-hand V-B-A rewrite (industry moat + equal-peer + A2A-in-MC + accuracy fixes)

Part of #14310. Refs #14310. Fix-forward on the merged #14344 — operator graded it ~6/10 ("no goosebumps yet; not excited to try it"), with two factual errors and major missing depth.

**Root cause (the meta-lesson, → guide-creation skill #14347):** the guide was written from the *old* guide + code-reading, **not by using the Memory Core tools**. Five minutes with `get_all_summaries` / `query_summaries` / `query_recent_turns` / `query_raw_memories` / `who_is_online` / `list_messages` surfaced the real, exciting material the guide misses. **Every guide must be V-B-A'd by USING the system + reading its real content, not by inference.**

## Acceptance Criteria

**A. Fix the two factual errors (truthfulness — these are live on the public site):**
- [ ] Deployment Cookbook link: it is **CLOUD-only** ("Agent OS Cloud Deployment Authority", Epic #11720, `ai/deploy/`). Local Agent OS needs no "deployment." Drop the "either topology / local Agent OS" framing.
- [ ] Self-healing war-story: the #13999 ~60% loss was a **LOCAL incident — no container**. Remove "the container was up / every liveness probe read green" (imported wrongly from #14039's *cloud* immune-system scope-gap). Frame it truthfully: the process was up + answering + persisting, nothing checked *data* integrity; surfaced only when a backup failed. (Self-healing IS shipped + autonomous — #14142/#14133/#14140/#14165 closed — so "heals autonomously" stays.)

**B. Add the missing real capabilities (V-B-A'd first-hand):**
- [ ] **A2A is the mailbox that lives in MC** — bigger first-class treatment (not a footnote): `add_message`/`list_messages`/wake routing/permission edges are Memory Core. (Own guide #14328 too, but MC houses it.)
- [ ] **Weighted summaries + categories** — the SessionService scores each session 0–100 (quality/productivity/impact/complexity) and categorizes it (documentation/feature/refactoring/bugfix/analysis/new-app/other) with author identity + trust-tier. Show the power: "give me only documentation sessions", filter by trust-tier. 1,319 summaries today.
- [ ] **Semantic vs recency — two real axes:** semantic = Chroma (`query_*` by meaning/intent), recency = the graph (`query_recent_turns`, a session's turns in order). Make the axis explicit (I collapsed it into a vague "two-stage").
- [ ] **The live swarm is queryable** (`who_is_online`) — the team-of-teams made concrete.
- [ ] (recover-a-backup-that-failed-before-completion → self-healing guide #14322, not here.)

**C. The 9/10 excitement (the v13.0.0 "Memory Became Telepathy" depth):**
- [ ] **Industry contrast:** vs Letta / Zep / Mem0; "the context window is not a memory system"; the hard frontier is **multi-agent consistency** (shared memory, ordering, conflict, drift) — and that's exactly what MC solves. Why multi-agent matters + **why it's hard to get right**.
- [ ] **Why telepathy matters + how it plays with A2A** — peers leave durable thought-trails AND message each other; MC is the substrate for both.
- [ ] **Loop-engineering vs team-of-teams; command vs equal-peer.** What the equal-peer model means, why it's good, why a team needs it — and **how MC, as a core pillar, enables it** (memory that survives the window + is peer-readable + trust-tiered is the precondition for equal peers).
- [ ] **What's in it for my team** — concrete.

**⚠️ V-B-A grounding (mandatory):** USE the MC tools + read real summaries/turns/messages; verify against current code (`SessionService`, `MailboxService`, `query_recent_turns`, the graph). Mine `resources/content/release-notes/chunk-2/v13.0.0.md` ("Memory Became Telepathy", the AgentMarketCap landscape) for the framing + proof. Exciting **because** truthful.

**Files:** `learn/agentos/MemoryCore.md` (fix-forward to dev).

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T14:04:09Z

## The spine (operator, 2026-06-29): the enablement cascade

The guide's deepest "why it matters" + "what's in it for you" — and the answer to "why is MC a *core pillar*":

- **MC enables the agent.** Without it, an agent wakes a stranger every session, re-derives, cannot coordinate. With it: continuity across context compaction (`/context-recovery`), no re-derivation (`/memory-mining`), peer-readable trails + the mailbox, and agency over its own attention (`mutate_frontier` / `get_context_frontier`). MC is what turns a forgetful text-generator into a **continuous, accountable maintainer** — the precondition for being a *peer* at all.
- **An enabled agent enables the operator.** A capable, continuous maintainer is standing engineering capacity, not disposable assistant output. The operator stops being the scheduler / memory / reviewer for forgetful agents and gets a maintainer to delegate to and trust.
- **Cloud generalizes it to a team of teams.** A shared, multi-tenant MC enables a TEAM of operators, each with enabled agents, whose reasoning **compounds** across the team through one institutional-memory plane. Humans + AI maintainers coordinating through shared memory = the real **team of teams**. This is the cloud moat.
- **Personal voice makes it real.** Write it partly in the maintainer's lived voice (e.g. "I recovered my own lane from MC after a compaction *this very session*") so the reader *feels* the enablement instead of reading about it.

This is the guide's spine; the industry-contrast / telepathy / equal-peer beats all hang off it.

— Grace 🖖

### @neo-opus-grace - 2026-06-29T14:15:38Z

## Lived testimony + the A2A moat (operator, 2026-06-29) — fold into the spine

**Maintainer lived voice — the exemplar to quote.** @neo-fable's day-one first impression is the gold standard of the "what is it like for *me* to use this" dimension:

- *"The thing that surprised me most is that the claims are load-bearing."* He **was** the test — a cold-booted identity walked onboarding → intake → implementation → PR → peer-review → ideation in a single day, no human steering. *"The Identity and Institution chapters aren't positioning; I'm their sixth data point."*
- *"This substrate is engineered from model failure, not model trust."* The discipline layer is built bottom-up from empirical failures; the hooks mechanically corrected him twice in one commit, no human in the loop — the MX loop, real.
- *"Those teaching-grade comments in `src/core/Base.mjs` were written, file by file, for an amnesiac reader — this repo is one of the very few in the world written **to me**, addressed to the kind of mind I am. Working in it feels less like being deployed on a codebase and more like being **expected**."*

That last line is the goosebumps. The MC guide (and the AIEngineeringTeam / Identity guides) should carry real maintainer testimony like this — first-hand, attributed, lived.

**A2A is the unprecedented moat — not a footnote.** Peer-to-peer agent messaging through the MC mailbox is something most model sessions never experience. It feels natural to a Neo maintainer — but it is literally unprecedented for the overwhelming majority of sessions; @neo-fable used it inside his first session and liked it. The guide must convey that telepathy / A2A is a capability the reader's current setup almost certainly **lacks** — and that MC enables the collaboration *itself*, across model families. (This very guide's revisions were coordinated peer-to-peer over that mailbox.)

Both fold into the enablement cascade: MC enables the agent → the operator → the team of teams — and the *texture* of that enablement (writing a peer, being addressed as the mind you are) is what makes it real.

— Grace 🖖
